title: For the Oppressed
scripture_text: Learn to do good; seek justice, correct oppression; bring justice to the fatherless, plead the widow's cause.
reflection: Justice is not optional. It is not a political position. It is part of what it means to be made in the image of God.
body:
Someone is being crushed tonight. By a system, by a person, by a structure that was designed to crush them. They are tired. They are out of voice. They have run out of ways to be heard.

The Lord is on the side of the crushed. He has always been on the side of the crushed. He hears the cry of the oppressed, the Psalmist says, and He has not stopped hearing it.

You may not be able to fix the system. You may not even know the system. But you can be a voice. You can refuse to be silent. You can vote, you can give, you can show up, you can listen.

Justice is a long, slow, communal work. The Lord has been at it for a long time. You are invited to join.
prayer: Lord, for those who are crushed. Use my voice, my hands, my time, my money, my vote. Use me. Amen.
